2025.name: Hack Week #1 — Hack me if you can
lessonsLearned: First hack week. Allowed solo projects — later changed to teams-only for Jan 2026.
4-day format (Mon kickoff, Tue-Wed build, Thu demos). Felt compressed.
Winners announced weeks later at Town Hall + Slack. Next time: announce faster.
Feedback survey sent — good practice to continue.

2026.name: Hack Week #2
lessonsLearned: Holiday scheduling was painful. MLK Day + BLR Republic Day created conflicts. Needed async schedules across offices (SF Mon-Fri, BLR Tue-Mon).
Flight/hotel budgets for remote employees were too tight on short notice. Increase buffer or book earlier.
Cross-functional team requirement (no more than 50% from same eng team) drove better collaboration.
8 teams participated, strong output. 'Teams shipped full products within three days. Not prototypes.' (Rishabh's LinkedIn post)
Mid-week demos + dinner was a great energy boost.
Productionization guidance on Day 4 was helpful. Several teams needed to scope down for v1.
Winner announcement delayed because Bharat and Shreyas were both out for scheduled Town Hall. Lock in announcement date and ensure judges available.
Loom recordings essential for async visibility across offices and non-engineering staff.
Late-night dinner policy (originally 8pm, adjusted to 6pm) was well-received.

2026.name: Hack Week #3

events: Set up shared Spotify Jam playlist
Run demo sessions per office (SF and BLR)
Post shipping guidance: (1) Productionize, (2) Support with PR reviews, (3) Record Loom once shipped
Help scope down to MVP if needed
Ensure all Loom links added to spreadsheet

tasks.text: Pick the dates — Avoid US and India holidays. Check holiday calendar, OOO schedules, BLR office holidays.
tasks.lesson: Jan 2026: Holiday conflicts with MLK Day and BLR holiday caused last-minute schedule changes.

tasks.text: Decide on cross-office schedule — If BLR and SF need different start days, plan async but parallel schedule
Confirm the daily structure — Recommended: Build (2 days), Demo (mid-week), Optimize/Ship (2 days)
Get budget approved — Talk to Evelyn about prizes, travel, team dinners/happy hours, daily lunches, Ramp card limits
Decide on prizes and categories — Past categories: Best Use of AI, Highest Customer Impact, Best Internal Tool / Most Useful DevTool. ~$1,000 per winning team.
Confirm judges — Typically Shreyas, Rishabh, Bharat + leadership. Ensure available for demo day AND deliberation.
tasks.lesson: Jan 2026: Both Bharat and Shreyas were absent for scheduled Town Hall announcement.

tasks.text: Assign a BLR logistics lead — Kunal or Sriram for coordination, Shiksha for logistics support
Create the #hack-week-[month-year] Slack channel
Draft and send kick-off announcement in #dev and #general. Have Bharat post.
Create team sign-up spreadsheet (Google Sheet)
Send reminder nudges
Draft Slack announcement templates
Set team formation rules — Teams of 2-4, no solo projects, cross-functional (>50% cannot be from same eng team), PMs/Designers encouraged
Set deadline for team/idea submission (~1 week before)
Have Bharat/Shreyas review and approve all ideas (2-3 days between submission and lock date)
Lock all teams and ideas (2-3 days before hack week)
Arrange travel for remote employees
tasks.lesson: Jan 2026: $400 flight budget and $1,400 hotel budget were too tight. Budget $550-600 for flights, $350/night for hotels in SF.

tasks.text: Coordinate BLR travel with Kunal/Sriram
Arrange daily lunches for SF and BLR offices
Plan social events — team dinner (mid-week after demos) and happy hour (end of week). Both offices.
Set up late-night dinner policy — teams working past 6pm can expense dinner on Fermat
Cancel all internal meetings for hack week
Set up on-call schedule for customer-facing coverage
Block calendars with hack week events
Set up Loom recording workflow
Host kick-off session
Post opening message in #hack-week channel
Confirm attendance
Encourage daily updates
Offer office hours (Bharat/Shreyas)
Set up Spotify Jam playlist
Run demo sessions per office
Record all demos
Host team dinner after demos
Post shipping guidance
Check in on productionization
Ensure Loom links in spreadsheet
Host end-of-week happy hour
Judges review all demos and Looms (2-3 days)
Pick winners across all categories
Announce winners at Town Hall — lock date in advance, ensure judges available
Have winning teams re-present (polished) to whole company
Distribute prizes — $1,000 per winning team, split among members
Post hack week recap in #general with all team names, Loom links, winners
Create LinkedIn content — coordinate timing with marketing
Feature hack week at offsite/company events if applicable
Send feedback survey (Google Form)
Hold planning retro — Sophie + Bharat + BLR leads
Update this checklist with new lessons learned
Track which projects made it to production (2-4 weeks later)

rubric.description: Originality, feasibility, and potential impact of the project concept
Architecture, reliability, test coverage, and production-readiness
Clarity, engagement, and polish of the demo presentation

prizeCategories.category: Best Internal Tool / Most Useful DevTool

teamFormationRules: Cross-functional: more than 50% cannot be from the same engineering team
PMs and Designers encouraged to participate

budget.travelNote: Budget $550-600 for flights, $350/night hotels in SF per Jan 2026 lesson
budget.foodNote: Daily lunches for SF and BLR offices + team dinner + happy hour

schedulingTips: January works well but watch for MLK Day and BLR Republic Day (Jan 26).
June works well but watch for July 4th proximity.
December is risky — holiday season makes it hard to get clean 5-day window. Push to early January instead.
